key_points: Electricity is the flow of electric charge through a conductor
An electric circuit is a closed path through which electric current flows
A simple circuit consists of a cell, wires, a switch and a bulb
If the circuit is open, current cannot flow and the bulb will not glow
Conductors allow electricity to pass through them while insulators do not
Switches are used to open or close an electric circuit
examples: Simple circuit: Cell → Wire → Switch → Bulb → back to Cell
When the switch is ON (closed circuit), the bulb glows
When the switch is OFF (open circuit), the bulb does not glow
Examples of conductors: Copper wire, aluminium
formulas: Electric circuit → A closed conducting path for electric current
Closed circuit → Current flows and device works
Open circuit → Path is broken and current does not flow
exam_tips: Remember the components of a basic electric circuit
Understand the difference between open and closed circuits
Learn examples of conductors and insulators
Practice drawing simple electric circuit diagrams
